name: tdmcp
description: AI-native visual creation for TouchDesigner — build real node networks from plain language.
long_description: tdmcp turns plain-language prompts into real, wired, laid-out TouchDesigner node networks. It ships an embedded knowledge base of TouchDesigner operators and Python classes plus 300+ tools spanning whole systems (audio-reactive, feedback, generative), building blocks (connect, animate, control panels, external I/O), and atomic node CRUD, and follows a create → verify → preview loop that checks for post-cook errors and captures a preview of the output. It runs entirely on your own machine, collects no data, and has no telemetry or background network calls: its normal activity is local HTTP to your own TouchDesigner bridge (default 127.0.0.1:9980), and the only other outbound connections are ones you explicitly trigger — importing a shader from Shadertoy or a URL you provide, or the optional AI copilot talking to the LLM endpoint you configure (local by default). See the privacy policy for details.

TDMCP_TD_HOST.title: TouchDesigner host
TDMCP_TD_HOST.description: Hostname/IP of the TouchDesigner Web Server DAT bridge. Use 127.0.0.1 when TouchDesigner runs on this machine.

TDMCP_TD_PORT.title: TouchDesigner port
TDMCP_TD_PORT.description: Port of the TouchDesigner Web Server DAT bridge (must match the bridge's port).

TDMCP_BRIDGE_TOKEN.title: TouchDesigner bridge token
TDMCP_BRIDGE_TOKEN.description: Optional bearer token shared with the TouchDesigner bridge. Leave blank only on trusted local networks.

TDMCP_RAW_PYTHON.title: Raw Python tools
TDMCP_RAW_PYTHON.description: Set to off to hide the two client-authored raw-Python escape-hatch tools.

TDMCP_TOOL_PROFILE.title: Tool profile
TDMCP_TOOL_PROFILE.description: Use full for all tools or safe to hide destructive/raw-code tools for autonomous sessions.
